description: Add versioned mobile API contract with bearer token authentication, FCM device registration, assignment event model, and backend tests. This does not break existing web clients because it adds new endpoints under /api/mobile/v1/.
steps: Add new models to prisma/schema.prisma: (a) MobileRefreshToken with fields: id (cuid), userId, tokenHash (String), deviceId (String for installation ID), platform (String: 'android'|'ios'), expiresAt (DateTime), revokedAt (DateTime?), createdAt. Index on userId and tokenHash. (b) DeviceToken with fields: id (cuid), userId, platform (String), installationId (String unique per device), token (String for FCM token), lastSeenAt (DateTime), createdAt, updatedAt. Unique constraint on [userId, installationId]. Index on token. (c) AssignmentEvent with fields: id (cuid), eventId (String unique, idempotency key = 'assign-{orderId}-{partnerId}-{timestamp}'), orderId, partnerId, orderNumber (String), assignedAt (DateTime), acknowledgedAt (DateTime?), fcmSent (Boolean default false), fcmSentAt (DateTime?), createdAt. Index on [partnerId, acknowledgedAt] and [orderId].
Run 'npx prisma migrate dev --name add_mobile_auth_and_fcm_models' to create the migration.

Create lib/mobile-auth.ts with: (a) generateAccessToken(userId, role) - signs a short-lived JWT (15 min) with claims {sub, role, iat, exp} using AUTH_SECRET. (b) generateRefreshToken() - crypto.randomUUID(). (c) hashRefreshToken(token) - bcrypt hash. (d) verifyAccessToken(token) - verifies JWT, returns decoded payload or null. (e) authenticateMobileRequest(request: Request) - extracts Bearer token from Authorization header, verifies it, returns {userId, role} or null.

Create app/api/mobile/v1/auth/login/route.ts - POST endpoint accepting {phone, otp} or {email, password}. For phone+OTP: normalize phone, call verifyLatestOtp, if valid create access+refresh tokens, hash and store refresh token in MobileRefreshToken with deviceId from request body. For email+password: validate against User passwordHash like staff-credentials provider. Return {accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn: 900, user: {id, name, role, phone}}. Apply rate limiting.

Create app/api/mobile/v1/auth/refresh/route.ts - POST accepting {refreshToken, deviceId}. Look up the hashed token in MobileRefreshToken where not revoked and not expired. If found, revoke it, create a new refresh token (rotation), create new access token, return both. If not found, return 401.

Create app/api/mobile/v1/auth/logout/route.ts - POST accepting {refreshToken} or {deviceId: 'all'}. Revoke matching refresh tokens and delete associated DeviceToken records. Requires valid access token in Authorization header.

Create app/api/mobile/v1/devices/route.ts - POST for registering/updating FCM device token. Accepts {installationId, platform, token}. Upserts DeviceToken by [userId, installationId]. Requires valid mobile access token. GET returns user's registered devices.

Create app/api/mobile/v1/assignments/route.ts - GET returns unacknowledged AssignmentEvents for the authenticated delivery partner (where acknowledgedAt is null). POST accepts {eventId} to acknowledge - sets acknowledgedAt only if partnerId matches the authenticated user (ownership check). Make acknowledgment idempotent (re-acknowledging returns success).

Modify app/api/admin/orders/[id]/delivery/route.ts to also create an AssignmentEvent record when assigning a partner. Generate eventId as 'assign-{orderId}-{partnerId}-{Date.now()}'. This is additive - existing SSE and push behavior remains.

Create tests/mobile-auth.test.ts with tests: (a) generateAccessToken creates valid JWT decodable by verifyAccessToken. (b) verifyAccessToken rejects expired tokens. (c) authenticateMobileRequest extracts token from Authorization header. (d) hashRefreshToken and comparison works.
Create tests/assignment-events.test.ts testing: (a) idempotent acknowledgment (acknowledging twice returns success). (b) ownership check (partner A cannot acknowledge partner B's event).
Commit with message 'feat: add mobile API v1 with token auth, device tokens, and assignment events'.
acceptance_criteria: npx prisma validate passes with new models
New API routes exist under app/api/mobile/v1/auth/, app/api/mobile/v1/devices/, app/api/mobile/v1/assignments/
verification: Run 'npx prisma validate' and confirm success
Run 'npm test' and confirm all tests pass including new ones
Run 'npm run build' and confirm build succeeds
findings: All new tests pass (8 mobile-auth + 5 assignment-events). Pre-existing test failure in tests/scroll-preservation.test.ts (test 21: root elements allow native scrolling) confirmed not caused by these changes. npm run build succeeds with all new routes visible in output. prisma validate passes. The test 'root elements allow native scrolling' fails because touch-action: pan-x pan-y is set on :root in the CSS - this is a pre-existing issue unrelated to mobile API work.
